location.office_time_required: Mondays, Tuesdays, and Thursdays (Anchor Days); additional days possible

level_signals: 5+ years of experience in highly analytical, fast-paced environments
Independently leading multi-month initiatives in ambiguous environments
Base salary range $185,000–$200,000 consistent with Senior IC in SF/NYC
Executive presence and influencing leadership decisions

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of experience in highly analytical, fast-paced environments (e.g., strategy & ops, finance, consulting, investment banking, or similar quantitative roles)
Strong first-principles problem solving and structured thinking, with ability to break down ambiguous business problems into clear analytical frameworks
Advanced analytical and modeling skills, including ability to conduct complex analysis in Excel/Google Sheets, SQL, or AI-assisted tools (e.g., Claude Code)
Experience designing dashboards, KPI frameworks, or performance reporting systems that drive business decisions and executive visibility
Extreme ownership mindset with track record of independently leading multi-month initiatives in ambiguous environments
Strong cross-functional communication and executive presence, with ability to influence across Sales, Customer Success, Finance, Data, and Product teams

Strong systems thinking with ability to identify inefficiencies and design scalable process improvements; you don't need to be an AI expert, but you're curious and willing to adopt AI tools to work smarter and deliver better results.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Notion power user or desire to learn -- prior Notion experience is not required, but you have the ability and desire to become a pro
Experience in a PLG or hybrid PLG + sales-led go-to-market environment
History of promotions or progressive responsibilities at work
Exposure to AI-enabled workflows or automation tools in a business context (e.g., using AI to streamline reporting, research, or operational processes)

scope.surface: Central GTM Strategy & Operations — global go-to-market performance management, strategic analytics, and operating systems across Sales, Customer Success, Marketing, Finance, Data, and BizTech
primary_metrics_inferred: Headcount allocation and coverage model effectiveness
core_responsibilities: Own core GTM performance reporting including forecasting, QBRs, Board updates, weekly business reviews, and ad-hoc updates
Lead high-impact analyses that shape how we invest and grow, including market expansion, segmentation, coverage models, headcount allocation, and productivity optimization

Drive high impact GTM initiatives from problem definition through execution, including forecasting redesigns, funnel efficiency improvements, post-sales target & incentive strategy, territory and motion design, quota strategy, sales profile design, and AI-enabled transformation efforts
Design and continuously improve the operating cadence, KPI frameworks, target methodologies, and cross-functional planning systems for Sales & Post-Sales
Identify inefficiencies and build scalable solutions using automation and AI-enabled workflows to reduce manual work, accelerate insights, and increase leverage across GTM teams

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned alongside cash compensation and benefits but form not specified

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly stated ($185K–$200K base for SF/NYC)
Equity mentioned as part of package
Clear scope of ownership — high-visibility role reporting into GTM leadership
Encourages applicants who don't meet every bullet to still apply
AI curiosity welcomed without requiring deep expertise — accessible bar
Role spans both strategy and execution, offering breadth
red_flags: Hybrid policy requires 3 designated Anchor Days per week (Mon, Tue, Thu) in-office — effectively near-onsite, not traditional hybrid
Visa sponsorship not addressed in the JD — unclear for international candidates
Compensation range qualified as SF/NYC-specific; other locations may vary downward
No mention of direct reports, but scope is very broad — potential for high expectations on a solo contributor
#LI-Onsite tag in JD may indicate fully onsite expectations vs. the described Anchor Days policy — mild inconsistency

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + years + scope of independence)
scope.business_model (inferred from PLG mention in nice-to-haves and Notion's known GTM model)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not enumerated as metrics in JD)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred as false from Anchor Days requirement and #LI-Onsite tag)
ambiguities: JD is tagged #LI-Onsite but describes a hybrid policy (Anchor Days Mon/Tue/Thu). Treated as hybrid here given explicit body language, but near-onsite in practice.
Compensation range is explicitly stated for SF and NYC only; no guidance given for other locations, and it's unclear whether other locations are eligible.
Equity is mentioned generically ('highly competitive cash compensation, equity, and benefits') without specifying form (RSUs, options, etc.).
